documents.title: Loudspeaker and Noise Pollution Deadlines
documents.content: CONDITION: Use of loudspeakers, DJ systems, or public address systems at any outdoor event. REQUIREMENT: Loudspeakers are strictly prohibited between 10:00 PM and 6:00 AM. During permitted hours, ambient noise levels must not exceed 65 dB in commercial areas and 55 dB in residential areas. A specific Police NOC is required for any sound system usage. EXCEPTION: Specific notified festival days where the deadline is extended to 12:00 Midnight.

documents.title: Police NOC for Crowd Assembly
documents.content: CONDITION: Any public gathering, commercial event, or ticketed show expecting more than 500 attendees. REQUIREMENT: Organizers must apply for a Police Assembly NOC at least 7 days prior to the event. The application must include a detailed crowd management plan, number of private security personnel deployed (ratio of 1 guard per 100 attendees), and a designated point of contact for emergencies.
documents.source_label: State Police Act - Public Order Guidelines

documents.title: Fire Extinguisher Ratio for Pandals/Structures
documents.content: CONDITION: Erection of temporary structures, pandals, or closed tents covering an area of more than 50 square meters. REQUIREMENT: Organizers must install at least one 9-liter water/foam fire extinguisher and one 4.5kg ABC powder fire extinguisher for every 50 square meters of covered area. All decorative materials used in pandals must be treated with fire-retardant solutions.
documents.source_label: National Building Code (Fire and Life Safety)

documents.title: Fire NOC for Pyrotechnics and Fireworks
documents.content: CONDITION: Use of fireworks, stage pyrotechnics, or open flames (like large cooking setups) at the event. REQUIREMENT: A mandatory Special Fire NOC is required. Fireworks are strictly prohibited within 100 meters of silent zones (hospitals, educational institutions). A dedicated fire tender must be stationed on-site for events using stage pyrotechnics.

documents.title: Road Closure and Traffic Diversion
documents.content: CONDITION: Events requiring partial or full closure of a public street, or moving processions that will occupy more than 25% of the road width. REQUIREMENT: Applications for road closures must be submitted to the Traffic Department 15 days in advance. Organizers must publish the approved traffic diversion plan in two local newspapers 48 hours before the event. Volunteers in reflective jackets must be deployed at all diversion points.

documents.title: Procession and Rally Routing
documents.content: CONDITION: Moving events like marathons, religious processions, or political rallies. REQUIREMENT: The exact route coordinates must be submitted. Processions are not allowed on major arterial highways during peak traffic hours (8:00 AM - 11:00 AM and 5:00 PM - 8:00 PM). Emergency lanes (minimum 3 meters wide) must be kept clear at all times for ambulances.

documents.title: FSSAI Licensing for Food Stalls
documents.content: CONDITION: Any event where food and beverages are sold or distributed commercially via temporary stalls. REQUIREMENT: Every individual food stall vendor must possess a valid FSSAI Registration Certificate. The event organizer is responsible for ensuring compliance. Potable drinking water and covered waste bins must be provided at every food stall.

documents.title: Solid Waste Management and Cleanup Deposit
documents.content: CONDITION: All outdoor public events expecting over 100 attendees on municipal or public grounds. REQUIREMENT: Organizers must pay a refundable cleanup deposit to the Municipal Corporation. Source segregation (wet and dry waste) is mandatory. If the venue is not cleared of all solid waste within 12 hours after the event concludes, the deposit will be forfeited and additional penal charges will apply.

documents.title: Public Restroom Requirements
documents.content: CONDITION: Events lasting more than 4 hours with an expected crowd exceeding 300 people where permanent facilities are insufficient. REQUIREMENT: Organizers must provide mobile/temporary toilets. The ratio is 1 toilet per 100 attendees, with a mandatory 50/50 split for male and female facilities. At least one accessible toilet for persons with disabilities must be provided.

documents.title: Private Indoor Event Exemptions
documents.content: CONDITION: Private, invitation-only gatherings (like weddings or private parties) held entirely inside licensed commercial venues (e.g., banquet halls, hotels) with no spillover onto public streets. REQUIREMENT: These events do not require separate Police or Traffic NOCs, provided the venue has a valid operating license and the crowd does not exceed the venue's stated maximum capacity.

documents.title: Application SLA and Timelines
documents.content: CONDITION: Standard application processing timelines for the Uttsav platform. REQUIREMENT: Standard applications must be submitted at least 15 days before the event. The departments are bound by an SLA to process and provide a decision (Approve/Reject/Query) within 7 working days of application submission.

documents.title: Silent Zones Restriction
documents.content: CONDITION: Events taking place near hospitals, educational institutions, or courts. REQUIREMENT: Areas within 100 meters of hospitals, educational institutions, and courts are declared Silent Zones. Use of loudspeakers, beating of drums, and bursting of firecrackers is strictly prohibited at all times in these zones.

documents.title: Medical First Aid Post Requirement
documents.content: CONDITION: Events categorized as High Risk or any event expecting a crowd of over 1000 people. REQUIREMENT: A dedicated First Aid post must be established on-site. For crowds exceeding 2000, at least one Advanced Life Support (ALS) ambulance and one registered medical practitioner must be stationed at the venue for the entire duration of the event.

documents.title: Structural Stability Certificate
documents.content: CONDITION: Erection of large stages, grandstands, or multi-story temporary viewing platforms. REQUIREMENT: Organizers must submit a Structural Stability Certificate issued by a government-approved structural engineer before the event commences, verifying that the stage can safely bear the expected load.
